translations.text: he/she doesn’t listen to me
if on the 15th day the Moon-god dies (= eclipses), the Great-King will die
he/she closes the gate
If the Moon-god hasn’t yet risen
They will restore the old ritual for old Ištar
And he/she closes / will close the door
If/when the gods acknowledge / recognize me
The chief of the cooks libates into the fireplace once
The deaf man closes / will close the windows
I offer / will offer one cow and 48 sheep to the deity
(But) when I arrived in Tarkuma, I burnt Tarkuma down to the ground.
The enemy did not flee.
He fled away before me
He fled away from Maraššantii̯a
And you will not show them the road to Ḫatti-land
Telipinu chased them away
But/and we will attack (the city of) Kapapaḫšuu̯a
I have just interrogated them
My Majesty has ordered Kallu as the Lord of the Cavalry
But then the enemy attacked (the city of) Zikkatta
The gods have allotted many years to me, the King
The Fate-Goddess has treated you badly
The King is sitting in/on the wagon
He is hit by the Storm-god
The King cleansed / purified himself

text: kat-ta-ma tu-el DUMU^MEŠ^-_KA am-me-el DUMU-_I̯A pa-aḫ-ša-ri
title: Example 3.06

translations.text: But/and further, my son will protect your sons
A heavy kunkunuzzi-stone fell down from the sky

text: ma-a-an-kán an-tu-uḫ-ša-aš la-ga-a-ri na-aš-ma-aš-kán ^GIŠ^GIGIR-az kat-ta ma-a-uš-zi
title: Example 3.08

translations.text: If a person falls over, or (if) (s)he falls down from a wagon
You have become a wolf

text: ma-aḫ-ḫa-an-ma-za _A-BU-I̯A ^m^mur-ši-li-iš DINGIR^LIM^-iš ki-ša-at ŠEŠ-I̯A-ma-za-kán ^m^NIR.GÁL _A-NA ^GIŠ^GU.ZA _A-BI-ŠU e-ša-at am-mu-uk-ma-za _A-NA _PA-NI ŠEŠ-I̯A EN.KARAŠ ki-iš-ḫa-ḫa-at
title: Example 3.10

translations.text: (But) when my father Muršili became a god (= died)
my brother Muu̯atalli sat down on the throne of his father
I became head of the army for (in service of) my brother
He/she is heard / is being listened to
Then they made them blind.
And we will take care of the gods on the ritual feasts.

text: nu-za ^URU^pal-ḫu-iš-ša-an-na tar-aḫ-ḫu-un nu URU-an ar-ḫa u̯a-ar-nu-nu-un
title: Example 3.14

translations.text: I conquered (the city of) Palḫuišša as well, and I burnt the city down to the ground

text: ma-a-an-mu DINGIR^MEŠ^ ka-ni-iš-ša-an-zi LUGAL-ez-zi-aḫ-ḫa-ri tu-uk-ma am-me-ta-az SIG5-eš-zi
title: Example 3.15

translations.text: when Gods elect me and I will become king it will become good to you from me
You will eat bread and drink water
They took each other by the hand
It does not lie on this tablet of the oath

text: ku-it-ma-an-za ú-e-eš nu-u-a ^URU^ḫa-at-tu-ši e-šu-en LÚ^MEŠ^ ^URU^_KA4-AŠ-KA4-ma iš-ta-ma-aš-ša-an-zi
title: Example 4.04

translations.text: While we were still present in Ḫattuša, the Kaškaeans were (litt. “are”) listening

text: nu-un-na-aš an-ni-ša-an-pát na-ak-ki-e-eš a-aš-ša-u-e-eš e-šu-u-en nu-un-na-aš le-en-ki-i̯a-aš e-šu-u-en
title: Example 4.05

translations.text: In the past we were important and good
we were (men) of the oath

text: ma-a-an ^URU^ḫa-at-tu-ši šal-li-iš u̯a-aš-ta-a-iš ki-ša-ri na-aš-šu-za LUGAL-uš na-aš-ma MUNUS.LUGAL-aš DINGIR^LIM^-iš ki-ša-ri
title: Example 4.06

translations.text: If in Ḫattuša a large disaster will take place
(i.e., if) either the king of or the queen will become a god (= dies)
(But) our head spins like a potter’s wheel

text: ^d^UTU-uš dam-me-iš-ḫa-an-da-aš ku-ri-im-ma-aš-ša an-tu-uḫ-ḫa-aš at-ta-aš an-na-aš zi-ik
title: Example 4.08

translations.text: You, Sun-god, are father (and) mother for suppressed and bereft persons
And I will eat you fully
Now I will eat and drink
(But) in Kaška, the locust have eaten the grain (harvests)
If, however, you will protect (= obey) the words of this tablet
Why don’t you eat nor drink?
The king will offer to the gods (specifically) these ones thirteen times through the window

text: nu LUGAL-uš a-ki _I-NA ^GIŠ^GU.ZA-ŠU-ma-za-kán ta-ma-iš ku-iš-ki e-ša-ri
title: Example 4.15

translations.text: The king will die, and someone else will sit down on his throne

text: ták-ku LÚ-aš da-me-da-ni A.ŠÀ A.GÀR an-da a-ki
title: Example 4.16

translations.text: If a person dies inside another field...
They have renewed his years and his awe
My father brought us together and made us swear an oath

text: nu KUR ^URU^pár-ḫa-an-na _IŠ-TU ^GIŠ^TUKUL e-ep-zi nu-kán a-pa-a-aš-ša _A-NA LUGAL KUR ^URU.d^U-TA-AŠ-ŠA a-aš-ša-an-za
title: Example 5.03

translations.text: and that (land) too will then be belonging to the King of the Land of Tarḫuntašša.
He will take the Land of Parḫa, too, with weapons
They seized him and they killed (him) together with his sons

text: _A-BU-I̯A-ma-an-na-aš-za ^m^mur-ši-li-iš 4 DUMU^ḪI.A^ ^m^ḫal-pa-šu-lu-pí-in ^m^NIR.GÁL-in ^m^ḫa-at-tu-ši-li-in ^f^DINGIR^MEŠ^.IR-in-na DUMU.MUNUS-an ḫa-aš-ta nu-za ḫu-u-ma-an-da-aš-pát EGIR-ez-zi-iš DUMU-aš e-šu-un
title: Example 5.05

translations.text: My father Muršili fathered us, four children, Ḫalpašulupi, Muu̯attalli, Ḫattušili, and Maššannauzzi, a daughter
And I was the youngest child of all.

text: nu-mu ^d^IŠTAR GAŠAN-I̯A ku-it ka-ni-iš-ša-an ḫar-ta ŠEŠ-I̯A-i̯a-mu ^m^NIR.GÁL a-aš-šu ḫar-ta
title: Example 5.06

translations.text: Because Ishtar, My Lady, had recognized / elected me
my brother Muwatalli held me dear
Now (s)he will stand / stands on / near the altar
I will either kill them or deport them

text: ki-i _ṬUP-PU ar-ḫa ḫar-ra-an e-eš-ta na-at _A-NA _PA-NI ^m^_MA-AḪ-ḪU-ZI _Ù _A-NA ^m^ḪAL-U̯A-LÚ ú-uk ^m^du-da-aš EGIR-pa ne-u̯a-aḫ-ḫu-un
title: Example 5.09

translations.text: This tablet was fully destroyed.
And I, Duda, made it new again before the eyes of Mahhuzi and Halwaziti.

text: nu É.DINGIR^LIM^ im-ma 1-an ḫar-ak-zi ^URU^ḫa-at-tu-ša-aš-ma LUGAL-aš a-aš-šu _UL ḫar-ak-zi
title: Example 5.10

translations.text: One temple indeed will perish
but the possessions of the King of Hattuša will not perish
They destroyed all of it fully!

text: pár-ku-i-ša-aš a-pa-a-aš mi-iš-ri-u̯a-an-za-aš a-pa-a-aš ḫar-ki-ša-aš a-pa-a-aš na-aš-kán ḫu-u-ma-an-da-az a-ša-nu-u̯a-an-za
title: Example 5.12

translations.text: She is pure, that one; she is beautiful, that one; she is white, that one, and she is provided with everything
(But) afterwards / then the enemy killed him.
They sweep the temples.
(But) Kupanta-LAMMA has sworn these things opposite / in the presence of / standing before Madduwatta

text: zi-ik-ma-za DUMU-aš nu _Ú-UL ku-it-ki ša-ak-ti na-aḫ-šar-nu-ši-ma-mu _Ú-UL
title: Example 6.01

translations.text: You’re a child and you know nothing
and you don’t impress me
They oppressed them and they died.
They opened the gates

text: ma-aḫ-ḫa-an-ma LUGAL-uš _I-NA É^TIM^ GAL a-ri ḫa-an-te-ez-zi-az-ma ^LÚ.MEŠ^ALAN.ZU9 ka-ru-ú a-ra-an-ta-ri
title: Example 6.04

translations.text: But when the king arrives at the palace
the clowns are already standing at the front

text: nu ke-e-ta-aš _A-NA KUR.KUR^ḪI.A^ šu-me-en-za-an É^ḪI.A^ DINGIR^MEŠ^-KU-NU ku-e e-eš-ta na-at LÚ^MEŠ^ ^URU^GA-AŠ-GA ar-ḫa pí-ip-pé-er
title: Example 6.05

translations.text: Which temples of yours were present in these lands
the men of Kaška have fully destroyed them

text: nu-kán NAM.RA^ḪI.A^ ku-iš GU4 UDU an-da e-eš-ta na-an ḫu-u-ma-an-da-an e-ep-ta
title: Example 6.06

translations.text: Which deportees, cows and sheep (= living booty’) were present inside
that he seized in its entirety
He calls all the gods by name
He oppressed the city in that way

text: ma-an-kán ^d^UTU^_ŠI^ _BE-LÍ-I̯A _BE-LU ku-in-ki pa-ra-a na-it-ti ma-na KUR-i LÚ.KÚR _Ú-UL dam-mi-iš-ḫa-ez-zi
title: Example 9.01

translations.text: Were you, My Majesty, My Lord, to send some lord,
the enemy would not be able to do damage to the country
They began shedding our blood
When the wagon goes to his house
the horse servant will give the spears to the gatekeeper
But later I, Anitta, Greatking, brought our God from Zalpuwa back to Neša
I am telling it to *you*, my God

text: nu-mu-za am-me-el DINGIR-I̯A DUMU.NAM.U19.LU tu-ug-ga-aš-ta-aš iš-ta-an-za-aš-ta-aš ÌR-KA ḫal-za-it-ta
title: Example 9.06

translations.text: You, god of mine, have called me, a mortal, as a servant of your body and your soul

text: nu mi-e-ú-uš ku-i-uš GU4^ḪI.A^-uš ^d^UTU-uš tu-u-ri-i̯a-an ḫar-ši nu-uš-ma-aš ka-a-ša DUMU.NAM.LÚ.U19.LU-aš ḫal-ki-in šu-uḫ-ḫa-aš
title: Example 6.09

translations.text: Which four oxen you, Sun-god, have harnessed
to them the mortal has just now strewn grain
They bring forth the deity out from the temple

text: nu-un-na-aš ^d^UTU ^URU^PÚ-NA GAŠAN-I̯A ku-e-da-ni pé-di ti-it-ta-nu-uš na-at tu-el a-aš-ši-i̯a-an-ta-aš DUMU-aš _ŠA ^d^IM ^URU^NE-RI-IK _AŠ-RU
title: Example 6.11

translations.text: In which place you, O Sun-goddess of Arinna, My Lady, have installed us
That is the place of your beloved son, the Storm-god of Nerik

text: nu-za-kán _A-NA ^GIŠ^GU.ZA _A-BI-I̯A ku-u̯a-pí e-eš-ḫa-at nu-za ke-e a-ra-aḫ-zé-na-aš KUR.KUR^MEŠ^ ^LÚ^KÚR _I-NA MU.10.KAM tar-aḫ-ḫu-un na-at-kán ku-e-nu-un
title: Example 6.12

translations.text: When I sat down on the throne of my father
I conquered these enemies in the foreign lands in a period of ten years
and I slew them

text: ma-a-an _I-NA UD.15.KAM ^d^_SÎN-aš a-ki a-ru-na-aš a-aš-šu ḫar-ak-zi na-aš-ma šu-up-pa-la-an te-pa-u-e-eš-zi
title: Example 6.13

translations.text: When on the 15th day the Moon-god dies, the goods of the sea will perish
or the cattle will diminish
They arrived at the place of the meeting

text: na-aš-ta an-da-i̯a _Ú-UL ku-in-ki tar-na-i pa-ra-a-i̯a-kán _Ú-UL ku-in-ki tar-na-i
title: Example 7.01

translations.text: He neither lets anyone in, nor lets anyone out

text: nu _I-NA KUR ^URU^TÁG-GA-AŠ-TA pa-a-un nu-mu iš-ta-ma-aš-ša-an ku-it ḫar-ke-er nu-uš-ma-aš-kán nam-ma UD.KAM-az GÉŠPU-it EGIR-pa-an-da _Ú-UL pa-a-un nu GE6-az i-i̯a-aḫ-ḫa-at
title: Example 7.02

translations.text: I went to the land of Taggašta. And because they had heard me
after (that) I did not go anymore to them with weapons at day-time
(but) I marched at night

text: ták-ku GU4-aš A.ŠÀ^ḪI.A^-ni ku-el-ka4 a-ki _BE-EL A.ŠÀ 2 GU4 pa-a-i
title: Example 7.03

translations.text: If a cow dies in someone’s field
the owner of the field will give two cows (in return)

text: ma-na-aš-kán šu-uḫ-ḫa-az kat-ta ma-uš-ta-at na-an ^MUNUS.MEŠ^SUḪUR.LÁL e-ep-per na-an _Ú-UL tar-né-er
title: Example 7.04

translations.text: She would have fallen down from the roof, (but) the SUḪUR.LÁL servants grabbed her
and did not let her go

text: nu ^d^UTU^_ŠI^ ^URU^kat-ḫa-id-du-u̯a-an GUL-un na-an _IŠ-TU NAM.RA GU4 UDU ^URU^KÙ.BABBAR-ši ar-ḫa ú-da-aḫ-ḫu-un URU-an-ma ar-ḫa u̯a-ar-nu-nu-un
title: Example 7.05

translations.text: I, my Majesty, attacked the city of Katḫaidduu̯a
and I brought it together with (its) inhabitants, cows and sheep (= all living booty) to Ḫattuša
But the city (itself) I burnt down to the ground

text: nu-kán ku-iš UDU da-ma-a-aš-zi ku-iš-ma ŠAḪ.TUR pé-e ḫar-zi _A-NA EZEN4 _DI12-ŠI
title: Example 7.06

translations.text: The one will drive the sheep, and the other will hold the piglet ready, on the festival of spring
I make figurines and place them in a basket

text: na-aš-ta tu-uk ^m^ḫu-ul-la-an ku-u̯a-pí gi-im-ma-an-ti pa-ra-a ne-eḫ-ḫu-un nu-ut-ta a-pí-i̯a _Ú-UL iš-ta-ma-aš-še-er
title: Example 8.02

translations.text: Where I had sent you, Ḫulla, in winter
there they did not listen to you
“It disappears (or) it stays”
The king will go from Taḫurpa into Arinna by wagon

text: na-aš _A-NA DUMU.É.GAL GÙB-li-it ŠU-it pa-a-i DUMU.É.GAL-ma-aš GÙB-la-az ki-iš-ša-ra-az LUGAL-i pa-a-i
title: Example 8.05

translations.text: He will give them to the palace servant with the left hand
and the palace servant will (then) give them with the left hand to the king

a-ap-pa-ma-kán ^m^at-ta-ri-iš-ši-i̯a-aš LÚ ^URU^_A-AḪ-ḪI-IA-A ar-ḫa ú-et nu EGIR-an tu-uk-pát ^m^ma-ad-du-u̯a-at-ta-an ku-na-an-na ša-an-ḫi-iš-ke-et

ka-a-aš-ma-u̯a ERÍN^MEŠ^ ^URU^_ḪA-AT-TI ^URU^ḫi-in-du-u̯a za-aḫ-ḫi-i̯a pa-it nu-u̯a-aš-ma-aš KAŠKAL-an pé-ra-an e-ep-ten nu-u̯a-ra-aš u̯a-al-aḫ-ten
nu-uš-ša-an ERÍN^MEŠ^ ^URU^_DA-LA-U-U̯A KAŠKAL-ši pa-ra-a ú-u̯a-te-e-er nu ú-e-er an-ze-el ERÍN^MEŠ^-_ti KAŠKAL-an e-ep-per nu-uš ni-ni-in-ke-er
na-aš-ta ^m^ki-iš-na-pí-li-in ^m^pár-ta-ḫu-ul-la-an-na ku-e-ner ^m^ma-ad-du-u̯a-at-ta-aš-ma-aš-ma-aš-kán pa-ra-a ḫa-aḫ-ḫar-aš-ke-et
title: Madduwatta-text

translations.text: Later came Attarišši̯a, the man from Ahhiyawa
and afterwards he repeatedly tried to kill you, Madduwatta
but when the father of my majesty heard of this
he sent Kišnapili, army and сharioteers to battle against Attarišši̯a
and then you, Madduwatta, did not withstand Attariššii̯a
and you yielded to him
and Kišnapili came from the land of Hatti to save you
and Kišnapiliš went to battle against Atariššia
and of Attariššii̯a he placed a hundred wagons and x thousand troops for battle
and they battled
and they killed one officer of Attariššii̯a
and of us they killed one officer, Zidānza
and Attariššii̯a turned away from Madduwatta
and he went to his land
and then they installed Madduwatta as a ruler the second time
later, the city of Dalauwa took up enmity
and Madduwatta sent the following message to Kišnapili
I will go to Dalauwa to attack
and y'all must go to Hinduwa
and I will attack Dalauwa
and then the army of Dalauwa will not come to help Hinduwa
and you will destroy Hinduwa
and Kišnapili brought the army for battle to Hinduwa
and Madduwatta then did not go to Dalauwa to battle at all
and he indeed sent a message to the men of Dalauwa instead
just now the army of the Land of Hatti went to battle to Hinduwa
and you must take the way in front of them
and you must attack them
and the army of Dalauwa moved forth on the way
and they came and seized the way of our army
and they took us by surprise
and they killed Kišnapili and Partahula
and moreover, Madduwatta repeatedly made fun of them

^m^ú-du-ub-šar-ri-i̯a-aš NIN-ZU ^m^ke-eš-ši-iš DAM-an-ni da-a-aš
MUNUS-aš _ŠUM-še-et ^f^_ŠI-IN-TA-LI-ME-NI mi-iš-ri-u̯a-an-za-aš ḫu-u-ma-an-da-az-zi-i̯a

ḪUR.SAG^MEŠ^-ša-aš nam-ma ḫu-ur-nu-u̯a-an-zi _Ú-UL pa-iz-zi na-aš-ša-an pa-ra-a _A-NA DAM-ŠU-pát _IŠ-ME
AMA-ŠU _A-NA ^m^_KE-EŠ-ŠI me-mi-iš-ke-u-u̯a-an da-iš DAM-KA-pát-u̯a-ták-kán a-aš-ši-i̯a-at-ta-at
nu-u̯a nam-ma ḪUR.SAG^MEŠ^-aš ḫu-ur-nu-u̯a-an-zi _Ú-UL pa-a-i-ši nu-u̯a-mu _Ú-UL ku-it-ki
ú-da-at-ti ^m^ke-eš-ši-iš-za ^GIŠ^ŠUKUR ša-ra-a da-a-aš UR.TUR^ḪI.A^-i̯a-za EGIR-an-še-et ḫal-za-iš
na-aš _I-NA ^ḪUR.SAG^_NA-TA-RA ḫu-ur-nu-u̯a-an-zi pa-it DINGIR^MEŠ^-eš-kán ^m^ke-eš-ši-i̯a
iš-pa-an-du-uz-zi še-er kar-tim-mi-i̯a-u-u̯a-an-te-eš nu-uš-ši ḫu-u-i-tar
ḫu-u-ma-an pé-ra-an ar-ḫa mu-un-na-a-er
^m^ke-eš-ši-iš ITU.3.KAM-aš ḪUR.SAG^MEŠ^-aš an-da ú-e-ḫa-at-ta EGIR-pa-ma-aš-ša-an URU-ZU
ša-an-na-pí-liš nu-u-ma-an pa-iz-zi ka-a-aš-ti ka-ni-in-ti nu ^m^ke-eš-ši-in
i-da-a-la-u̯a-an-za GIG-an-za ḫar-zi
title: The story of Hunter Kešši

translations.text: and he went to mount Natara for hunting
But the gods were angry with Kešši because of the (absence of) libation
And they fully hid all the game before him
K. roams around in the mountains for 3 months
But he doesn't want to go back to his city empty-handed, in hunger and in thirst
An evil illness holds Kešši
Kešši took the sister of Udubšarri into wifehood
The woman’s name is Šintalimeni
She is beautiful and provided with everything
And Kešši only listened to his wife
Kešši no longer takes care of the gods with thick-bread and libations
He no longer goes to the mountains for hunting
He only listened to his wife
His mother started speaking to Kešši
You no longer go to the mountains for hunting
Only your wife has become dear to you
You don’t bring me anything!
Kešši took up his spear
And called his dogs behind him

title: Annals of Muršili

translations.text: When I had restored order in the land of Karkamiš
I came upwards out of the land of Karkamiš
and I came into the land of Tegaramma
And when I arrived in (the city of) Tegaramma
Nuu̯anza, the general, and all lords came to meet me in Tegaramma
and they found me in (there)
I wanted to go to the city of Ḫai̯aša
But because the year had become (too) short
And the lords spoke to me
“The year is becoming (too) short for you”
“Our Lord, do not go to Ḫai̯aša!”
I did not go to Ḫai̯aša
I then went to Ḫarrana
and my army arrived in Ḫarrana
and I performed an inspection of the army there
Because the city of Ii̯aḫrešša and the land of Piggainarešša were an enemy towards me
I went to Ii̯aḫrešša
I made the days into nights
and I drew the troops in haste
and I, the Greatking, marched with the troops and cavalry secretly
The mighty stormgod, My Lord, had called (the deity) Ḫašammili, My Lord, for me
and he held me hidden
No-one saw me
and I proceeded to attack the land of Piggainarešša in (its) sleep
I then found (it) together with the living booty
and I took it
and I fully burnt down the land of Piggainarešša
The next day I came back to (the city of) Ii̯aḫrišša
and I burnt down to the ground Ii̯aḫrešša the city
and took (it) together with its living booty
The next day I marched forth to (the city of) Taptina
When I arrived in Tarkuma
I burnt Tarkuma down to the ground.
And the men of Taptina, the men of Ḫaršama (and) the men of Pikurza came to meet me,
and they kneeled down on (their) knees for me,
and they spoke as follows
"Our Lord, do not destroy us!”
“But take us into servanthood”
Make us into troops and charioteers
We will be going on campaign alongside you!
And I took them in servantship
And I made them into troops and charioteers
The next day I went forth
and I burnt down Ḫāišeḫla and Kantiššiša
Then I went back to Ḫakpišša
And I came back to Ḫattuša
And I spent the winter in Ankuu̯a
And when I arrived in (the city of) Tegaramma
